(1 comment)

I ran it as $ "# ./lustre/tests/auster -C sanity"

and it looks like mgs/mds was not mounted when auster is trying to mount the client. Tried both on single node and multinode.

<snip>
++ combined_mgs_mds
+++ mdsdevname 1
++++ mds1_HOST=rockky85
++ cd /root/lustre-release/lustre-release
++ LUSTRE=/root/lustre-release/lustre-release/lustre
++ bash -c 'mount -t lustre -o user_xattr,flock rockky85@tcp:/lustre /mnt/lustre'
mount.lustre: mount rockky85@tcp:/lustre at /mnt/lustre failed: Input/output error
Is the MGS running?
+ return 5
+ return 1
<snip>

Patch Set 2:

The `-C` option won't work unless the filesystem is already setup. `llmount.sh` will setup and filesystem then mount. But the mount shouldn't be necessary, only the setup. It you use an external filesystem (MGS/MDS/OSS running on different nodes), '-C' should work without mounting it first.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> The `-C` option won't work unless the filesystem is already setup. `llmount.sh` will setup and filesystem then mount. But the mount shouldn't be necessary, only the setup. It you use an external filesystem (MGS/MDS/OSS running on different nodes), '-C' should work without mounting it first.

Can you please add this little info. Maybe under auster (usage) section ? So, new users are clear of the workflow. Otherwise your code looks good. Thanks

<snip>
 -C, --client-only     Only run client-side sanity tests. Users should define
                       FSNAME and mgs_HOST manually. (Requires FS to be already setup)
<snip>

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Tim,

Could you give a quick example of how to actually run client only tests?  Like what the config and invocation would be?  This is a useful bit of functionality, I know I'd use it when re-testing stuff since it's way faster to reload a client than a whole server

Maybe just stick it in the Jira?  And if there's a short version maybe in the commit message?

Patch Set 3:

There's an example in the auster help text. I usually use something like:

 sudo FSNAME=myfilesystem mgs_HOST=1.2.3.4 ./lustre/tests/auster -v -C sanity --only 1

I use it to run quick sanity tests against real production filesystems. All you need to do is setup the client. I run something like:

 sudo ./lustre/tests/llmount.sh -l

to load the modules. Of course, I run everything from my dev tree.

Patch Set 3:

> I use it to run quick sanity tests against real production filesystems.

Bold :)

Thanks, missed that.
Patch Set 3: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 0439aaadb1b640225d700aeb2ae894d07e9b3c5b by Oleg Drokin

parents.subject: LU-16883 ldiskfs: update for ext4-delayed-iput for RHEL9.0

commit.subject: LU-16936 auster: add --client-only option
commit.message:
LU-16936 auster: add --client-only option

Add flag to auster to run sanity tests only on the
client-side. This leverages some existing functionality
to avoid having to setup ssh to filesystem hosts and
some other tedious setup.

Force test-framework.sh to honor the --no-setup flag.
Several test suites attempt to setup Lustre even if
auster says not to. Some lower level tests, like those
related to OBD device loading, require Lustre to be
not setup.

Change some [ to [[ in test-framework.sh to silence
some error messages.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>
Change-Id: I24de10743c3845b51fe29518ffc993b15a7c2cdd
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/51509
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Arshad Hussain <arshad.hussain@aeoncomputing.com>
Reviewed-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
